beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check messages at home.
beats.reasoning: Monday morning start with full energy; Hazel is a pragmatic homebody who begins days deliberately. 30 min is tight but realistic for a weekday wake-up routine.
Light Rail closure between Westlake–U-District (9am–3pm) doesn't affect West Seattle to SoDo route; drive is ~20 min but budget extra for Monday traffic and parking.

beats.activity: Coach team scrimmage and review film before lunch.
beats.reasoning: Hazel coaches a competitive esports team; morning sessions are typical. She's aware of the USA–Wales FIFA match at 11am but isn't a soccer fan—work takes priority until it's time to step away.

beats.activity: Grab brunch at a casual downtown spot; light meal.
beats.reasoning: 11am is brunch time; Hazel steps away from work briefly. Salumi in Pioneer Square is a real, pragmatic choice—close to SoDo, doesn't require her to watch the FIFA match (she's not interested), avoids crowded sports bars.
Short walk/drive back; 30 min allows for leisurely pace and a brief detour if needed.

beats.activity: Afternoon coaching session, strategy meetings, and player feedback.
beats.reasoning: Core work block; typical Monday afternoon for a coach. Ends before evening to allow time to head home and decompress.
Evening commute; SoDo to West Seattle via bridge, ~25–30 min in light post-rush traffic. Sunny day, Hazel might enjoy the drive.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home; handwrite a letter to a close friend.
beats.reasoning: Hazel values quiet time and close friendships; writing letters by hand is her preferred way to connect. Dinner at home fits her homebody nature and Monday wind-down.

beats.activity: Paint or sketch in home studio; enjoy the evening light.
beats.reasoning: Sunny Monday evening with good natural light is perfect for painting—one of Hazel's key hobbies. Indoor activity fits pragmatic mood and avoids crowds.

beats.activity: Wind down: light reading, tea, and prepare for bed.
beats.reasoning: Pre-sleep wind-down at home ensures the agent is stationary before the sleep beat; 1.5 hours allows for a calm transition into night.
8-hour sleep overnight, ending at 06:00 next morning, spans the midnight boundary and completes a full, realistic day for a homebody coach.

diary: Monday morning came easy—coffee tasted better than usual, maybe because of the sunshine streaming through the kitchen. The commute to SoDo felt shorter, though I made a mental note about the Light Rail closure; glad I drove today. The scrimmage was sharp. Our mid-laner finally nailed that rotation we've been drilling for weeks, and I could see it click for the whole team. Grabbed brunch at Salumi on the way back—just a quick bite, nothing fancy, but the place had this buzzing energy. Everyone was talking about the World Cup match at 11. I thought about stopping by a bar downtown to catch it, but honestly, the afternoon sessions were more important. We needed to lock in the strategy before Friday's qualifier. Back home by mid-afternoon, I handwrote a note to Marcus—something I haven't done in months. The studio light was perfect for painting, so I spent the last stretch before dusk working on an old landscape. Tea and a book wrapped the day up quietly. Felt balanced.
thought_process: I skipped the World Cup watch partly because of the team's schedule—we're two weeks out from a big tournament—but also because I've learned that FOMO doesn't serve me anymore at 57. The Light Rail closure actually worked in my favor since I drive; I didn't have to reroute or stress. The team's progress today genuinely lifted my mood; seeing them execute what we've been building felt like the real win, more satisfying than any sports bar atmosphere. The handwritten letter was intentional—I've been meaning to reconnect with Marcus, and the sunny day, the good coaching session, all of it made me feel generous with my time. By evening, I needed the quiet studio time to decompress. It's a rhythm I've found works: intense focus during work hours, then deliberate slowness at home.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
